Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal impairment. LDL and total cholesterol are high, increasing cardiovascular risk. Urine specific gravity is low, and urine is brown and cloudy, which may indicate underlying renal or hepatic pathology. HbA1c is low, but glucose is normal. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible chronic kidney disease and cardiovascular risk management.
